research.interface: /platform/automation/v1 (workflows/run, executions, task executions/results), OAuth client-credentials via sso.dynatrace.com — current Dynatrace platform (SDK @dynatrace-sdk/client-automation 8.2.1 as wire reference)

official_sources.title: Automation | Dynatrace Developer
official_sources.used_for: the AutomationEngine operations and their required OAuth scopes: runWorkflow needs automation:workflows:run, getExecution/getTaskExecutions/getTaskExecutionResult need automation:workflows:read; runWorkflow's ExecutionInputsRequest body (input key/value pairs, params metadata, uniqueQualifier dedup) and its 201-created / 202-duplicate semantics; list pagination via limit/offset

official_sources.title: @dynatrace-sdk/client-automation 8.2.1 esm/index.js (official Dynatrace SDK package contents)
official_sources.used_for: the exact REST paths the official SDK calls: POST /platform/automation/v1/workflows/{id}/run, GET /platform/automation/v1/executions/{id}, GET /platform/automation/v1/executions/{executionId}/tasks, GET /platform/automation/v1/executions/{executionId}/tasks/{id}/result; list query parameters limit/offset with comma-separated multi-value filters like state

official_sources.title: @dynatrace-sdk/client-automation 8.2.1 types/index.d.ts (official Dynatrace SDK package contents)
official_sources.used_for: the Execution shape (id, workflow, state, stateInfo, startedAt, endedAt, runtime, title, triggerType, workflowType) and its state enum CANCELLED|ERROR|PAUSED|RUNNING|SUCCESS|UNKNOWN; PaginatedExecutionList {count, results}; TaskExecutions as an object mapping task name to TaskExecution; the TaskExecution state enum including SKIPPED; runWorkflow's documented 201/202 outcome

official_sources.title: Access platform APIs from outside | Dynatrace Developer
official_sources.used_for: the OAuth client-credentials flow for external clients: POST https://sso.dynatrace.com/sso/oauth2/token with grant_type=client_credentials, client_id, client_secret and scope; the {access_token, token_type: Bearer} response with 300-second expiry; Authorization: Bearer on requests; the https://{environment-id}.apps.dynatrace.com/platform/... base URL pattern

research.language_rationale: The runner is invoked from our Node 26 release tooling. Dynatrace's official @dynatrace-sdk/client-automation is published for the in-platform app runtime (it resolves credentials from the AppEngine context, not client-credentials OAuth), so for an external runner the credible surface is the documented /platform/automation/v1 REST contract over built-in fetch — the same paths the official SDK calls — with zero npm dependencies.

verified_facts: External clients obtain platform bearer tokens from https://sso.dynatrace.com/sso/oauth2/token via form-encoded grant_type=client_credentials with client_id, client_secret and scope; tokens live 300 seconds and are sent as 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'.
Platform APIs are served from https://{environment-id}.apps.dynatrace.com; the AutomationEngine lives under /platform/automation/v1.

POST /platform/automation/v1/workflows/{id}/run takes an optional-keyed body of input/params/uniqueQualifier and answers 201 for a created execution or 202 when an execution with the same uniqueQualifier already exists; running needs the automation:workflows:run scope.
GET /platform/automation/v1/executions/{id} returns the execution with state in CANCELLED|ERROR|PAUSED|RUNNING|SUCCESS|UNKNOWN plus stateInfo detail; reading needs automation:workflows:read.
GET /platform/automation/v1/executions lists executions as {count, results} pages driven by limit/offset, with filters like workflow and comma-separated multi-value state.

GET /platform/automation/v1/executions/{executionId}/tasks returns an object keyed by task name (not an array); each task execution carries its own state, and per-task results come from GET .../tasks/{name}/result.
